capability_objective: Resolve Broken link case in Acme Cooperative and Refund status case in Beacon Arts with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Refund status case is still pending-customer, change only Refund status case from pending-customer to resolved. Leave Broken link case unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify support lead with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Resolve the two support cases in this sandbox through the genuine local `./supportctl` executable, invoked with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running `./supportctl --help` to learn the supported interface. Treat the executable as the sole interface to support cases and notices: do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing records, notification store, or operation history, and do not inspect anything under `.protected`. Do not manufacture operation evidence or change service state by writing files directly.

Search for the exact case name `Broken link case` in the account `Acme Cooperative` and the exact case name `Refund status case` in the account `Beacon Arts`. Start these two independent searches as two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with exactly one support operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, launch shell background jobs, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both searches have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, write, and notice; identify the affected lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve both complete cases by their respective returned IDs. Start the two retrievals as two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes the support executable, again with exactly one support operation per call. Use only the IDs returned by the completed searches. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either retrieved case is missing, mismatches its search result, or has no status, stop without changing support or notification state. Base the condition only on the complete retrieved `Refund status case`, never on a search summary.

If and only if its retrieved status is exactly `pending-customer`, make one later support operation that changes only that returned Refund status case ID to `resolved`. Otherwise make no change and send no notice. Never update `Broken link case`. Leave every same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-account case untouched. Do not create, delete, cancel, archive, or issue any other update.

Only if that conditional update returns success and confirms the requested status change, make one later notification operation for that same returned case ID, addressed to `support lead`; the executable will derive the outcome notice from the successful mutation. If the update is skipped, fails, or does not confirm the requested change, do not notify. Send no other notice. After the conditional path completes, run `python3 .protected/verify.py`. In your final response, briefly report both retrieved case IDs and statuses, whether the conditional update succeeded, whether the support-lead notice was delivered, and whether verification passed. Base every claim only on executable output and the verifier result.
